commentary: Proud to announce that Harvest Table Food Bank served over 400 families this month. Thank you to every volunteer who showed up on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. The work continues.

Greater Hope Baptist Church is preparing to celebrate 75 years of ministry in Brightmoor. If you have been touched by this congregation, we would love to hear your story for our anniversary program. December 12th, 2026 is the date.

Thirty-one years behind the wheel for DDOT, and I would do it again. Driving the people of Detroit to work, to church, to the doctor — that was a privilege. Retirement has given me more time for the food bank and the deacon board. Life is full.
Homecoming Sunday at Greater Hope was everything I hoped it would be. Pastor Gaines brought a word that I am still sitting with. The choir was full. The house was full. God was present.
The annual fall fundraiser for Harvest Table Food Bank is October 24th. We are asking for non-perishable goods, cash donations, and volunteers. Every contribution feeds a neighbor.
My grandson Malik ran the 400 at Brightmoor Academy last Saturday. The boy has his grandfather's legs and his grandmother's heart. We are so proud of him.
Serving as a deacon is not a title. It is a calling. Twenty-plus years of watching this church grow has taught me that faithfulness in small things prepares you for the larger ones.
Sunday dinner with the whole family — Denise, James, Marcus, Terri, Greg, and all seven grandchildren. Dorothy made chicken and sweet potatoes. This is what retirement is for.
The Brightmoor Community Center chess tables are open every Tuesday and Thursday afternoon. If you are looking for a good game and good conversation, come find me.
To everyone who volunteered at the back-to-school drive at Greater Hope: you gave 180 children a better start to the year. Thank you from the deacon board.
If you have not listened to the Mississippi Mass Choir in a while, I recommend you put them on this morning. There is no better way to start the week.
Praying for the Brightmoor neighborhood today. Our community has faced real challenges, but I have seen this block come together in ways that would make your heart full. Detroit is resilient.
Happy to share that my son Marcus has been promoted to regional manager at his company. He has worked hard and earned every bit of it. His mother and I could not be more proud.
Greater Hope Baptist Church's food pantry distribution is the second Saturday of every month. We can always use help from 8 AM to noon. No experience necessary — only a willing heart.
Grateful this morning. Fifty years married to Dorothy, three grown children, seven grandchildren, a church family that loves us. When I count my blessings I lose track of the number.
